mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2020-10-15 indicates maximum accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500K persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise and recession risk declines.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141,367K as of 2020-10-15 reflects post-COVID recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor rebound
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 500K fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 900K units annualized indicates strong residential construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence. This signals sustained aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as construction and materials sectors benefit.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 926K as of 2020-09-15 indicates above-threshold construction activity
failure_condition: HOUST > 900K fails to precede positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average, equity returns compress over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward and recession risk rises.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.18 as of 2020-10-15 reflects post-COVID manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines > 0.5% fail to precede negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 in absolute value over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the interaction amplifies confidence in sustained aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if either series weakens independently.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436,772M and HOUST at 926K both elevated as of 2020-09-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and construction
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate future rate hikes and equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.74 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2020-09-15 and 2020-10-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: The interaction of high inflation and low rates fails to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a Cox proportional hazards model over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power. When UNRATE crosses above this threshold, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk rises.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2020-10-15 is near threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and demand weakness
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% fails to precede negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 in absolute value over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at an annualized rate above 3% signals strong economic expansion. This always-on mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth accelerates and recession risk remains low throughout the expansion phase.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,304.8B as of 2020-07-15 reflects post-COVID recovery trajectory
regime_hint: any regime with positive real GDP growth
failure_condition: Positive real GDP growth fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 2-year window during expansion periods.
